basics.name: Igor Sadalski
basics.summary: Machine Learning Scientist at Cellular Intelligence. Working on multi-modal foundation models for life sciences.

work.summary: 6th engineer at a Harvard-MIT spin-off building multi-modal foundation models for life sciences ($60M+ raised, led by Khosla Ventures).
highlights: Co-authored publications at ICML, ICLR, and Nature Machine Intelligence on pre-training strategies and measurement noise scaling laws for single-cell foundation models.
Designed and implemented pre-training and post-training pipelines for billion-parameter transformers on terabytes of single-cell genomics data across 32 AMD MI325x GPUs using PyTorch FSDP and SLURM.
Developed a reasoning LLM via rejection sampling for data curation, SFT for cold-start, and GRPO with vLLM rollouts — improving automated biological annotation quality.
Wrote high-throughput fused Triton kernels (sparse-gate embedding, Softmax, LayerNorm) for single-cell genomics, reducing training memory overhead and increasing GPU utilisation.

education.thesis: Generative Modeling with Efficient Transformers for Pro-Active Bus Routing

publications.name: Large-Scale Benchmarking of Gene and Expression Encoding Strategies for Single-Cell Foundation Models
publications.publisher: ICLR 2026 Workshop on Generative AI for Genomics
publications.summary: First author. Presented at ICLR 2026 Workshop on Generative AI for Genomics.

publications.name: Scaling Laws for Noise for Cellular Representation Learning
publications.summary: Under review at Nature Machine Intelligence.

publications.name: Scaling Up Scaling Laws for Noise
publications.publisher: ICML 2025 Workshop on Multimodal Foundational Models for Life Sciences
publications.summary: Presented at ICML 2025 Workshop on Multi-modal Foundational Models for Life Sciences.

publications.name: Generative Modelling of Residuals for Real-Time Risk-Sensitive Safety with Discrete-Time Control Barrier Functions
publications.publisher: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA) 2024

teaching.summary: Contributed to the design of final-round problems for the Polish AI Olympiad.
Designed and delivered weekly machine learning and AI classes for community college students across the US.
Founded and led a team of 20+ students building autonomous driving software; coordinated perception, planning, and control, and competed on the Silverstone F1 track.

awards.title: Fulbright Scholarship
awards.summary: One of only three recipients in Poland (declined to work in industry).

awards.title: MSc Distinction
awards.summary: Highest classification for UK master's degree.

research.summary: Designed efficient transformer attention mechanisms for spatio-temporal demand forecasting, applied to shuttle bus scheduling on the Harvard campus.
Developed CVAEs and diffusion models to learn residual dynamics for drones, enabling tighter safety guarantees with discrete-time control barrier functions; resulted in an ICRA 2024 publication.
Accelerated model predictive control solvers by implementing custom matrix operations, reducing per-step computation time for real-time robotic control.

openSource.name: Stanford Biomni — General-Purpose Biomedical AI Agent
openSource.description: Implemented state-of-the-art foundation models (ESM-2, Transformer, scGPT, STATE) with standardized interfaces (Python, LangChain).

openSource.name: MIT Hackers Club (Sundai.ai) — Various Projects
openSource.description: Built a multiplayer LLM-powered game (Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind, Convex, Vercel).
